feature: Add EditorConfig and code formatting configuration
workflow_rationale: This is a simple configuration task that creates a single .editorconfig file with ktlint-compatible settings. No complex dependencies, no service integration, no API changes - just adding a standard configuration file following Kotlin/Android best practices.

phases.name: Configuration Setup
phases.description: Create .editorconfig file with Kotlin code style rules and ktlint configuration

subtasks.description: Create .editorconfig with Kotlin/Android code style rules
subtasks.notes: Successfully created .editorconfig file with Kotlin/Android code style rules. File includes 4-space indentation, UTF-8 charset, LF line endings, 120 char max line length, and ktlint-compatible settings. Verification passed. Committed in 0485e95.

subtasks.description: Add inline documentation to .editorconfig explaining configuration choices

documentation_requirements.header_comment: Explain purpose of EditorConfig for consistent code style
documentation_requirements.section_comments: Document why specific values chosen (4-space indentation, 120 char line length, etc.)
documentation_requirements.ktlint_reference: Link to Kotlin style guide and ktlint documentation

subtasks.notes:
Successfully added comprehensive inline documentation to .editorconfig explaining all configuration choices. Added detailed comments explaining:
- Purpose of EditorConfig and reference to Kotlin style guide
- Rationale for UTF-8, LF line endings, final newlines, and trailing whitespace
- Why 4-space indentation for Kotlin (official standard)
- Why 120 character line length (practical modern compromise)
- Specific explanations for each file type (Kotlin, XML, Gradle, JSON, YAML, Markdown)
- Technical details like POSIX requirements and git diff behavior
Verification passed. Committed in 9d7e43c.

parallelism.speedup_estimate: N/A - sequential subtasks in single phase

key_decisions.integration_approach: EditorConfig-based configuration that works with ktlint, IDE, and CI tools

future_integration.gradle_plugin: Task 001 or future build setup will add ktlint Gradle plugin
future_integration.ci_integration: Task 003 will use this configuration in CI lint checks
future_integration.ide_support: EditorConfig is natively supported by Android Studio and IntelliJ IDEA

verification_strategy.reasoning: This task only creates a configuration file with no functional code changes. The .editorconfig file is declarative configuration consumed by tools - it cannot break the application. Verification is limited to ensuring the file exists and contains expected properties.
acceptance_criteria: File includes documentation comments explaining choices
Configuration follows Kotlin official style guide

unit_tests.reasoning: Configuration file has no testable logic

steps: Open project in Android Studio/IntelliJ IDEA
Format a sample Kotlin file and verify 4-space indentation is applied
Verify no ktlint errors when running ktlint (once integrated in task 001)
acceptance_criteria: Configuration matches Kotlin official style guide

qa_signoff.approval_reason: All acceptance criteria met. Configuration is complete, correct, and production-ready.
